quality.rationale: ZAMO正交标架变换与对偶基求解，涉及矩阵求逆与物理背景明确

origin.question: Problem 17.18 (b) The “Zero Angular Momentum Observers” (ZAMO's) in the Kerr geometry have basis 1-forms$$\begin{array}{l} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {t}} = \left| g_{t t} - \omega^{2} g_{\phi \phi}\right|^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {d t} \\\tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = \left(\mathrm {g}_{\phi \phi}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \left(\widetilde {\mathrm {d} \phi} - \omega \widetilde {\mathrm {d t}}\right) \\\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathbf {r}}} = \left(\boldsymbol {\Sigma} / \Delta\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \overline {{{\mathrm {d} \mathbf {r}}}} \\\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\theta}} = \Sigma^{\frac {1}{2}} \widetilde {\mathbf {d} \theta} \\\end{array}$$where $\omega \equiv -\mathbf{g}_{t\phi} / \mathbf{g}_{\phi \phi}$(b) Find the dual basis vectors.
origin.solution: The dual basis $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\alpha}}$ is defined by $< \tilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{\alpha}},\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\beta}} > = \delta_{\beta}^{a}$ . If $\tilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\hat{\alpha}} = \mathbf{A}_{\beta}^{\hat{\alpha}}\tilde{\pmb{\omega}}^{\beta}$ and $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\beta}} = \mathbf{B}_{\hat{\beta}}^{\gamma}\mathbf{e}_{\gamma}$ , then the matrix $\mathbf{B}_{\hat{\beta}}^{\gamma}$ must be the transposed inverse of $\mathbf{A}_{\beta}^{\alpha}$ . To find $\mathbf{B}$ from A we can separate the t-phi piece from the (diagonal) r-0 piece:$$\left[ \begin{array}{c} \tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\mathfrak {t}}} \\\tilde {\boldsymbol {\omega}}^{\hat {\phi}} \end{array}\right] = \left[ \begin{array}{c c} | \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {tt}} - \omega^{2} \mathrm {g}_{\phi \phi} |^{\frac {1}{2}} & 0 \\- \omega (\mathrm {g}_{\phi \phi})^{\frac {1}{2}} & (\mathrm {g}_{\phi \phi})^{\frac {1}{2}} \end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{c} \widetilde {\mathrm {d} t} \\\widetilde {\mathrm {d} f} \end{array}\right]$$implies that$$\left[ \begin{array}{c} \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\mathbf {t}}} \\\mathbf {e}_{\hat {\boldsymbol {\phi}}} \end{array}\right] = \left[ \begin{array}{c c} | \mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {tt}} - \omega^{2} \mathbf {g}_{\boldsymbol {\phi} \boldsymbol {\phi}} |^{- \frac {1}{2}} & \omega | \mathbf {g}_{\mathbf {tt}} - \omega^{2} \mathbf {g}_{\boldsymbol {\phi} \boldsymbol {\phi}} |^{- \frac {1}{2}} \\0 & (\mathbf {g}_{\boldsymbol {\phi} \boldsymbol {\phi}})^{- \frac {1}{2}} \end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{c} \mathbf {e}_{\mathbf {t}} \\\mathbf {e}_{\boldsymbol {\phi}} \end{array}\right].$$Since the $r-\theta$ piece is diagonal, it is trivially inverted:$$\mathbf {e}_{\mathrm {\hat {r}}} = (\Delta / \Sigma)^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathbf {e}_{\mathrm {\hat {r}}} \qquad \mathbf {e}_{\hat {\theta}} = \Sigma^{- \frac {1}{2}} \mathbf {e}_{\theta}.$$
